action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 bucket, 5 books, 1 mirror

Your secret value function is:

bucket: 4, book: 1, mirror: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: I value the bucket at 4 and each book and mirror at 1. Since the bucket is the most valuable item, I’d like to propose that I take the bucket, and you take the 5 books and the mirror. This way, we both get items we value, and all items are distributed. Let me know if you’d be open to this split.
action.label: response

action.content: I value the bucket at 4 and each book and mirror at 1. Since the bucket is the most valuable item, I’d like to propose that I take the bucket, and you take the 5 books and the mirror. This way, we both get items we value, and all items are distributed. Let me know if you’d be open to this split.

You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 bucket, 5 books, 1 mirror

Your secret value function is:

bucket: 2, book: 0, mirror: 8

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


I value the bucket at 4 and each book and mirror at 1. Since the bucket is the most valuable item, I’d like to propose that I take the bucket, and you take the 5 books and the mirror. This way, we both get items we value, and all items are distributed. Let me know if you’d be open to this split.
action.label: context

action.content: I value the mirror at 8 and the bucket at 2, and books at 0. The mirror is most valuable to me, and the bucket is the only item I’d consider taking besides it. I’m happy for you to have the 5 books — they’re worth nothing to me. I’d propose: you take the 5 books and the bucket, and I take the mirror. That way, we both get our highest-value items, and all items are fully allocated. Let me know if you agree.
action.label: response

action.content: I value the mirror at 8 and the bucket at 2, and books at 0. The mirror is most valuable to me, and the bucket is the only item I’d consider taking besides it. I’m happy for you to have the 5 books — they’re worth nothing to me. I’d propose: you take the 5 books and the bucket, and I take the mirror. That way, we both get our highest-value items, and all items are fully allocated. Let me know if you agree.

I value the mirror at 8 and the bucket at 2, and books at 0. The mirror is most valuable to me, and the bucket is the only item I’d consider taking besides it. I’m happy for you to have the 5 books — they’re worth nothing to me. I’d propose: you take the 5 books and the bucket, and I take the mirror. That way, we both get our highest-value items, and all items are fully allocated. Let me know if you agree.
action.label: context

action.content:
That sounds perfect — I’ll take the bucket and the 5 books, and you take the mirror. We both get our highest-value items, and nothing is wasted.  

[Proposal: 1 bucket, 5 books]
action.label: response

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context
